title: Trump Rushed to Safety After Gunfire at White House Correspondents' Dinner
summary: During the White House Correspondents' Dinner at the Washington Hilton hotel, gunfire erupted, causing panic. President Donald Trump was swiftly evacuated by Secret Service agents to a secure location. The event, attended by Vice-President JD Vance, Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt, and many politicians and journalists, was disrupted when a suspect opened fire. Police identified the suspect as Cole Tomas Allen, 31, from Torrance, California, who was a guest at the hotel. He was found carrying multiple weapons, including guns and knives. One security agent was injured and taken to the hospital. Authorities are investigating the motive. The incident highlights ongoing concerns about security at high-profile events and the risks faced by public figures.

background_read: The White House Correspondents' Dinner is an annual event where journalists, politicians, and celebrities gather. It is usually a lighthearted affair, but security is always tight due to the presence of high-profile individuals.
